pi_items.source: Web search confirmed real physicians named Kenji/Kenichi Tanaka exist in US medical directories (US News Doctors, Doximity, etc.), but this specific instance with identifier DOC-2024-0047 is synthetic test data created by task setup scripts

Code inspection of setup_task.sh (line 19) and export_result.sh (line 12) confirm this identifier is used. OpenMRS documentation confirms custom identifiers are supported. Format DOC-YYYY-#### is custom and not from real provider registries.
Code inspection of verifier.py (line 35) confirms these are the name components matched during verification. They are the given name and family name components.
Confirmed in task_utils.sh (line 9), setup_bahmni.sh (line 13), and seed_bahmni.py (line 224). This is the standard OpenMRS URL within the Bahmni environment.

Confirmed in task_utils.sh (lines 13-14), setup_bahmni.sh (lines 14-15), and seed_bahmni.py (lines 226-228). These are default Bahmni/OpenMRS administrator credentials, confirmed by OpenMRS Talk forum discussions.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic healthcare provider in a Bahmni/OpenMRS environment. The provider name 'Kenji Tanaka' is a realistic name (real physicians with this name exist in US medical directories), but the specific identifier 'DOC-2024-0047' is custom test data. The provider is created via the OpenMRS REST API at https://localhost/openmrs using default Bahmni credentials (superman/Admin123). All test values are created synthetically by task setup scripts that ensure a clean state before agent execution.
